name: GPTCache (Semantic LLM Cache)

auth.notes: Local caching layer; integrates with existing LLM API auth

performance.notes: Cache hits return in ms vs seconds for LLM calls; semantic similarity matching adds ~5-20ms overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Semantic caching layer for LLM API calls. Cache hits for similar (not identical) queries using embedding similarity. Drops into OpenAI SDK with minimal code change. Configurable: storage backends (SQLite, Redis, Milvus, FAISS), embedding models, similarity threshold. Exact match and semantic match modes. Can reduce LLM API costs significantly for applications with repeated similar queries (customer service, FAQ bots). Inactive development since 2023.

gotchas: Semantic cache misses can return wrong cached responses if threshold too loose
Complex setup for production (needs vector store configuration)
No streaming support for cached responses
best_when: FAQ bots and customer service apps with repeated similar queries
Cost reduction for high-volume LLM applications
Development/testing to avoid burning API credits
avoid_when: Production systems needing active maintenance and support
summary: GPTCache adds semantic caching to LLM API calls — cache hits return cached responses for similar (not identical) queries using embedding similarity. Drops into OpenAI SDK with minimal changes. Configurable backends (SQLite, Redis, Milvus, FAISS) and similarity thresholds. Can dramatically reduce API costs for FAQ bots and repetitive query patterns. Development appears inactive since 2023 — consider alternatives for new projects.
